reasoning: The market placed the probability of an open-source AI model achieving a perfect score on the 2026 IMO at 0.40 in early April 2026. Recent developments show rapid progress: in late 2025, DeepSeekMath-V2 achieved a score of 35/42 (5 out of 6 problems) on IMO 2025, essentially matching the performance of proprietary models like Google DeepMind and OpenAI's systems. Furthermore, DeepSeek recently released a model (V3.2 Speciale) specialized for Olympiad-level math. The resolution criteria are highly permissive, allowing for arbitrary, closed-source scaffolding, parallel reasoning, and equivalent real-time limits. However, crossing the threshold from 35 out of 42 to a perfect 42/42 requires perfectly solving the most notoriously difficult problems (typically problem 6), which historically stumps both top humans and AI logic processes due to the vast combinatorial search space. Therefore, while plausible, it remains an uphill battle. Adjusting slightly downward from the market's 0.40 to reflect the extreme difficulty of a flawless IMO score, I estimate a ~0.38 probability for the nearest timeframe, scaling slightly toward 0.5 per instructions for the parsed resolution dates ("N", "/", "A").
